overViewOfOnline.subHeading: Lead Organizations with Integrated HR and Financial Expertise
overViewOfOnline.description: The MBA in Human Resource Management and Finance at Teksversity equips learners with essential HR skills and financial management capabilities required to support modern business functions. The curriculum covers people management, compensation systems, workforce analytics, budgeting, financial planning, and organizational finance. Through practical projects, learners explore employee lifecycle management, forecasting, financial reporting, and HR–finance decision frameworks. This online program is ideal for working professionals and graduates seeking flexible learning and dual-domain career opportunities in HR, finance, and business operations across India and global markets.
keyPoints: Understand HR systems, compensation, and workforce planning
Learn budgeting, costing, and financial analysis essentials
Strengthen analytical, managerial, and strategic skills
Apply ethical HR and finance practices in real settings
Prepare for HR, finance, and combined managerial roles

heading: Why Choose MBA in Human Resource Management and Finance
in Teksversity?
whyOnline.initialDescription: Teksversity offers a hybrid-focused MBA integrating HR practices with financial strategy to strengthen organizational performance. Students gain hands-on experience in HR policy, recruitment, payroll, budgeting, and financial reporting, preparing them for dual-functional roles in business ecosystems. The curriculum blends conceptual knowledge with practical case-based insights.

description.firstDescription: Expert faculty from HR and finance guide learners through compensation planning, performance systems, cost control, financial forecasting, and organizational planning. These real-world applications help students build confidence in making HR and financial decisions aligned with business goals.

description.secondDescription: With flexible scheduling, guided mentorship, resume building, mock interviews, and placement support, the program is ideal for working learners and fresh graduates. Teksversity’s industry exposure and skill-based learning modules prepare students for HR, finance, and hybrid managerial roles across sectors in India and abroad.

faq.question: What is an MBA in Human Resource Management and Finance?
faq.answer: An MBA in HRM and Finance is a dual-specialization postgraduate program that combines workforce management with financial decision-making. It focuses on organizational behavior, talent development, financial planning, corporate strategy, and performance management, enabling students to handle both HR and financial responsibilities in business environments.

faq.question: Who should pursue this course?
faq.answer: This program is ideal for graduates who want to build careers in HR leadership, financial analytics, business strategy, payroll management, or corporate administration. It suits individuals interested in managing people, optimizing resources, and contributing to business growth through financial insights.

faq.question: What skills will I gain from this specialization?
faq.answer: Students develop skills in recruitment, training and development, compensation management, labor laws, financial modeling, budgeting, investment analysis, risk management, and business analytics. The program also enhances communication, leadership, and strategic thinking abilities.

faq.question: What subjects are included in this specialization?
faq.answer: Key subjects include Organizational Behavior, Human Resource Planning, Talent Acquisition, Compensation Management, Corporate Finance, Financial Accounting, Investment Analysis, Risk Management, Business Law, and Strategic Management.

faq.question: What career opportunities are available after completing this MBA?
faq.answer: Graduates can work as HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Specialist, Training & Development Manager, Payroll Analyst, Financial Analyst, Business Consultant, Corporate Recruiter, Risk Analyst, Compensation and Benefits Manager, or HR Business Partner.

faq.question: How does this specialization benefit my career?
faq.answer: This dual specialization equips you to manage both human capital and financial resources—making you highly valuable to organizations. It opens doors to leadership roles, accelerates career growth, and increases employability across industries like IT, banking, manufacturing, healthcare, and consulting.

faq.question: What is the eligibility criteria for MBA in HRM & Finance?
faq.answer: Applicants must hold a bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognized university. Some institutions may consider entrance exam scores, academic performance, and interview evaluations during admission.

faq.question: Does the program include practical exposure?
faq.answer: Yes. Students participate in case studies, HR analytics projects, finance simulations, internships, and fieldwork assignments that develop real-world problem-solving and managerial skills.

faq.question: What tools and software will I learn?
faq.answer: The program may include training in HRMS tools, payroll software, Excel (Advanced), SAP HR/FICO, HR analytics tools, and financial modelling software used in corporate environments.

faq.question: What is the salary potential after completing this MBA?
faq.answer: Fresh graduates typically earn between ₹4–7 LPA, while professionals with experience or additional certifications can earn ₹8–18 LPA or more, depending on skills, industry, and job role.
